trivia_bullets.text: Tyrant's Machine was originally printed in Magic 2015 (M15, released July 18, 2014) and reprinted in Battlebond (June 8, 2018). Both printings use the same Yeong-Hao Han illustration and carry identical oracle text ({4}, {T}: Tap target creature) and flavor text. Scryfall confirms exactly two printings. The card is a colorless artifact with a generic 2-mana casting cost, making it format-agnostic — legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, Commander, and Pauper. [Scryfall API]

Flavor text: "Though tempered differently, all wills can be broken." — Inquisitor Kyrik. Inquisitor Kyrik is a named MTG character who appears only in this card's flavor text across both printings; no other canonical reference to Kyrik has been identified in official Wizards lore articles or story spotlights (research did not surface a named plane origin or broader appearance). The quote frames the device as an interrogation/will-breaking tool. [Scryfall API flavor_text field]

The activated ability ({4}, {T}: Tap target creature) is a slow, expensive tap effect — useful for neutralizing blockers or attacking creatures but widely considered underpowered compared to more efficient tap-down effects available in most formats. EDHREC rank of 27,372 reflects minimal Commander adoption. In Pauper, the card is legal but has never established a competitive presence. [Scryfall EDHREC rank field]

related_cards.note: The iconic tap-a-permanent artifact; functional ancestor of Tyrant's Machine at similar mana cost

related_cards.note: Similar tap/untap artifact for creature control

related_cards.note: Tap-effect artifact with combo potential, contrasts the utilitarian Machine
